title: Selliguea lanceola
intro: Selliguea lanceola, also known as the lance-leaf polypody, is a species of fern native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Selliguea lanceola include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Selliguea lanceola is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various ailments, particularly those related to the respiratory and digestive systems.
taxonomy: Selliguea lanceola is a member of the family Polypodiaceae, commonly known as the polypody fern family. It is a species of fern in the genus Selliguea. The genus Selliguea is part of the family Polypodiaceae, which is a group of vascular plants known as ferns. Selliguea lanceola is further classified into the class Polypodiopsida, which is the class of ferns. This classification system is used to group plants based on their evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics.

Its leaves are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. In some cultures, the plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce pain and swelling. Additionally, Selliguea lanceola is believed to have antimicrobial properties, which may help to prevent the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms.
